comment.body:
Alma, if we were correct through time around the monthly numbers. How can we know that the correction will be a “normal pullback” instead. 
I mean, correcting looking at the monthly numbers takes time, so one may miss a normal pullback, thinking it will mean revert using time correction. 
Is it not valid after X numbers of points from the calculated value or is there a way to know if it’s mean reverting through bigger pullbacks or through time?

comment.body:
To my understanding the market can find equilibrium through mean revert or time correction. You wrote about time correction an hour ago. Is there a way to see that the equilibrium is done through pullback instead of time correction?
Maybe how much it goes up/down of course. 
But for instance if we would drop 200 points from here, can one rule out time correction then (or that there are less odds for it )?

comment.body:
Hi Alma, more of a math question but I’m almost done with the first version on my model based on the educational posts you’ve made (literally running a parameter sweep scripts for optimization right now) but I’m curious about aspects of the vol surface on the options chain. 

First thought that comes to mind is concepts in differential geometry to find dislocations on the surface in order to find mis pricing in vol and therefore edge, but this seems too simple and I figure the market is efficient enough to arbitrage this away? I’m wondering if that’s the case or if you can still find these dislocation trade this way. 

Currently thinking of using hessian eigenvectors to locate curvature “hotspots” and structuring butterflies around them or exploiting regions on the surface with abnormally high surface curvature in the time dimension to build calendar spreads. 

Anything would be helpful here. Thank you and I hope you’re vacation is amazing
